message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
*Incident Number INC100097:* sev-1 order-confirmations are dropping
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
*Incident Number INC100067:* sev-1 user activity is dropped to 0
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
We're seeing a spike in failed transactions—about 40% of payment attempts are failing during checkout over the last few minutes.
Acknowledged on INC100067. I need specifics: how many customers are impacted, and which regions are affected? Also, what is the estimated time to resolution?
Uh oh...here we go again
@nishant.sarraff-zomato.com can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
seems like payment is down
:star: Qualify the problem
any changes done on payment side ?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
any changes done on our end ?
Any updates on the investigation?
Yes, I deployed a new version of CheckoutService to production about 1 hour ago, and Daniel deployed a PaymentService update 5 minutes ago, plus Tanya made a PaymentDatabase change 10 minutes ago.
I need numbers. How many users or orders are affected? Which regions are impacted? Is this a total outage or partial? Give me a timeline for resolution.
can we revert all the changes done very recently
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
I will rollback my checkout change right away
if we can revert them
wihtout any issue ?
Rollback complete!
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
Looks like the UK is particularly affected
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
Something else must be causing this
we are reverting the changes.
We need this fixed!
The new code isn't being invoked yet, can I investigate a bit more.
I really think my code is solid and all the unit tests passed, can we check the logs before rolling anything back?
If you want to rollback AdService or PaymentDatabase, I can do that, just let me know which one to start with.
rollback PaymentDatabase changes as well
Alright, rolling back the PaymentDatabase change now
Rollback complete.
Understood. While you revert, I need to know: how many UK customers or orders are currently impacted? Are we losing revenue right now? Keep me updated on progress and ETA for full recovery.
still seeing the error ?
:star: Reproduces the incident
Unfortunately, the payment failures in the UK are still at 100% even after the PaymentDatabase rollback.
what are the changes that we have reverted bob ?
So far, Shay's checkout service change and Tanya's PaymentDatabase change have both been rolled back, but the issue persists.
:star: Reviewed changes
we have reverted the recent changes but stilling seeing the issue
what else are left. only ads service changes right ?
Yes, the only other recent change is the AdService update, which I can also roll back if you want.
That's correct, the AdService change is the only one left unreverted from today's deployments.
Revert hasn't resolved it—what's the current impact in terms of lost orders or revenue? Is this still isolated to the UK? I need an ETA for when service will be restored.
also, tanya can we check if the issue happening because of any infra contention
:star: Facts over opinion
I've checked our infrastructure metrics and there are no signs of resource contention or anomalies at the platform level.
we are stilling seeing the issue for UK users
can we revert ads changes as well
Okay, rolling back AdService now
Rollback complete. This change was just cosmetic, so it's unlikely to be the cause.
Understood. Quantify the impact—how many UK users or orders are affected right now? Are we still losing revenue? I need concrete numbers and an updated ETA for resolution.
is site taking too much time to load ?
No, site load times are normal—it's just the payment step that's failing for UK users.
I see one change is still pending to be rolled back, which is into payments made by Daniel
daniel can you please confirm this
From my end, the site loads quickly and I don't see any latency issues, but the payment failures are still happening in the UK.

also shay and daniel 
check all your rollback once again
:star: Manage actions and timings
I've double-checked my rollback and can confirm the checkout service is back to the previous stable version.
I've reviewed my deployment and nothing has been rolled back on my end; my changes are still live and I still believe they're not the cause, but I'm open to further investigation.
can we revert those changes if they are backward compatible
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now
Rollback complete. Might take a few minutes to stabilise
Did that fix it?
working fine for me
Would you like me to place a test order to confirm everything is working as expected?
i just placed it and it was working
Do you want me to run another verification transaction just to be sure everything's resolved?
there was a change on checkout service
Noted. Focus on business impact: how many UK customers are unable to check out? What is the estimated revenue loss so far? I need a timeline for when this will be fixed.
we have rolled back all the changes now
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
not seeing any error

can shay 
please do thay
I'll check the payment service logs now.
I'll try a test transaction from my side.
Logs are looking clean, no more 504 errors.
My test payment went through successfully! Looks like we're back in business.
Looks like we're back in business.
daniel seems liek your Bitcoin payment service change were the cause
Glad we were able to pinpoint the issue. Bob, could you please confirm if customer metrics are back to normal on your end?
Thanks for confirming. I'll review the change in detail and make sure we address the root cause before any future deployments.
Confirm that transactions are processing and revenue is flowing as expected. Are all UK users able to complete orders now? I want verification that business operations are fully restored.
Shall we close the incident for now ?
tanya can you please share the impact as well

I can summarize the internal impact, but for customer-facing metrics and overall impact, Bob will have the most accurate data. Bob, could you please share the latest on customer impact and order success rates?
From a security perspective, there were no alerts or anomalies during the incident. For a full picture of customer impact, let's check with Bob for the latest external metrics.
bob, can you please share the data
Looking into it now... Metrics seem to be improving!
I don't think the Beatles would've anticipated cryptocurrency so let's keep it that way for now
Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally
Excellent! Get me a full incident report by end of day

Drill Wrap-Up

Great work everyone - crisis averted!
yes, all the UK users are avle to complete the orders now
got confirmation from Bob as well
Good. Thanks for the quick confirmation. Monitor closely and alert me immediately if anything changes.
we can mark this incident as complete now
